title: Business or Pleasure
author: Rachel Lynn Solomon
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Business or Pleasure" book by Rachel Lynn Solomon?
Options:
A. I don't dislike her. She's easygoing and relaxed, two words I've never used to describe myself. She's entertaining. He looks down. It's likely been like that for a while, right? I'll adjust it for you. I lean in as he willingly holds out his arms, and now my knee is also touching his. Should I roll both sleeves up or down? I ask hoping he says up. Up would give me more reasons to be close to him. Up, please, he says, blushing again. His skin is warm, his breathing steady. I take my time, ensuring his sleeves are the same length, each roll uncovering more freckles.
B. She doesn't bother me. She's easygoing and relaxed, two words I'd never use for myself. She's a fun person. He looks at his sleeves. They've likely been uneven for hours now, right? I'll adjust them for you. I lean in as he willingly extends his arms, and now my knee is pressed against his too. Should they be rolled up or down? I ask hoping for up. Having them up would let me be close to him more. Up, please, he says, blushing again. His skin is warm, his breath steady. I take care rolling up each sleeve, uncovering more freckles with every turn.
C. This version of myselfI dont hate her. Shes carefree and laid-back, two adjectives Ive never associated with myself. Shes fun. He glances down. Its probably been that way for hours, huh? Ill fix it for you. I lean in as he gamely holds out his arms, and now my knee is pressed against his, too. Do you want them both up or both down? I ask the question hoping for a specific answer. Up would give me more reasons to touch him. Up, please, he says, blushing again. His skin is warm, his breath even. I take my time, making sure his sleeves are the same length, each roll of fabric revealing more freckles.
D. I don't have any ill feelings toward her. She's carefree and laidback, two traits I've never seen in myself. She's fun to be around. He glances at his sleeves. They've probably been uneven for a while now, huh? Let me fix that for you. I move closer as he obligingly sticks out his arms, and now my knee is touching his too. Should I roll them both up or both down? I ask hoping he chooses up. Having them rolled up would allow me to be near him longer. Up, please, he says, blushing again. His skin feels warm, his breathing is calm. I slowly roll up each sleeve, revealing more freckles with each turn.
Answer:

Up would give me more reasons to be close to him. Up, please, he says, blushing again. His skin is warm, his breathing steady. I take my time, ensuring his sleeves are the same length, each roll uncovering more freckles.
